event: Saint Jerome raising Bishop Andrea from the dead
symbols: Jerome in cardinal-like robes — his traditional depiction as a Church Doctor
bishop rising from bed in mitre — the miraculous resurrection through saintly intercession
cardinal hat floating above — divine confirmation of Jerome's holiness and authority
bedchamber setting — the boundary between death and life where the miracle occurs
description: In an interior chamber, a figure in blue and red robes with a cardinal's hat approaches a bed where a bishop in white vestments and mitre kneels in prayer, apparently risen from the dead. A red cardinal's hat floats or descends from above in the upper portion of the scene. The background shows pale walls with subtle decorative elements. Other figures stand at left observing the miracle. The composition is formal and symmetrical, reflecting the solemn nature of the miraculous event.

_meta.title: St. Jerome who resurrected the bishop Andrea
